evidence.scientific_clarity: All 12 studies shown with CI lines and weight-scaled squares; red diamond for pooled FE estimate; dashed null line; x-axis labeled correctly — brief's claim is fully conveyed.
evidence.typography: Script sets no pdf.fonttype=42 or rcParams override; PDF embeds DejaVu Sans subsets (Type3/subset), not editable Type-42 outlines; default matplotlib font used throughout.
evidence.palette: Steelblue for studies and firebrick/darkred for pooled diamond is a reasonable directional distinction; palette is effectively two-color and CB-safe, but no explicit CB check and red is used for the pooled row (not a directional +/- signal), which is acceptable.
evidence.layout_density: Panel fills the space well; study labels are readable; pooled annotation at right edge clips slightly outside axes but is visible; y-range is tight with 0.7 padding; no major whitespace waste.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: No legend (none strictly needed for a forest plot); caption matches figure content and pooled value; title duplicates x-axis label concept; pooled annotation text is small (7.5 pt) and may be hard to read in print.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is generated but script does not set matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42, so text is stored as Type3 subset glyphs rather than selectable/editable outlines; SVG also saved without svg.fonttype='none'.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; brief asked for pdf+png+caption+script — all delivered.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the plot type, number of studies, marker scaling rule, pooled estimate with exact value and 95% CI [0.196, 0.294], and the null-effect line — specific numerical claim present.

overall_strengths: Complete forest-plot implementation: weight-scaled squares, CI lines, diamond pooled estimate, null-effect dashed line, and separator — all brief requirements met.
Caption is precise and self-contained, reporting the pooled log-OR (0.245, 95% CI [0.196, 0.294]) and explaining every visual element.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype='none') not set, so text in the PDF/SVG is stored as Type3 subset glyphs — not selectable or editable in Illustrator/Inkscape.
Default DejaVu Sans font used with no explicit rcParams configuration; small annotation text (7.5 pt) at the right edge may be illegible in a two-column journal layout.
